assertion_results.text: The plan explicitly references reusing common/src/db/query.rs::apply_filter for comma-separated multi-value query parameter parsing rather than writing new parsing logic (constraint 5.4)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan states: 'use apply_filter from common/src/db/query.rs to parse the comma-separated values and generate a SQL IN clause.' Reuse analysis: 'This avoids writing custom comma-splitting or SQL IN clause logic.'

assertion_results.text: The plan references following the filter pattern from modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/list.rs as a structural guide for implementing the license filter
assertion_results.evidence: Plan references 'following the same pattern as the severity field in the advisory list endpoint at modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/list.rs'. Reuse analysis provides full section on using advisory endpoint as structural template.

assertion_results.text: The plan references using entity/src/package_license.rs for the JOIN query rather than writing raw SQL or creating a new entity
assertion_results.evidence: Plan states: 'Join through the package_license entity (entity/src/package_license.rs) to filter packages by their declared license. Use SeaORM JoinType::InnerJoin.' Reuse analysis: 'No changes to the entity are needed — it already maps the required columns and relationships.'

assertion_results.text: The reuse analysis covers all 3 Reuse Candidates from the task description, explaining how each would be applied
assertion_results.evidence: Reuse analysis has three dedicated sections covering all candidates: (1) apply_filter, (2) advisory/endpoints/list.rs, (3) entity/src/package_license.rs. Summary table confirms: 'All three Reuse Candidates listed in the task description are applied.'

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections (constraint 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan modifies: package/endpoints/list.rs, package/service/mod.rs. Creates: tests/api/package_license_filter.rs. Additional files listed under 'Files to Inspect (read-only, not modified)'. No out-of-scope files modified.

assertion_results.text: The plan does not propose creating new utility functions that duplicate the functionality of apply_filter (constraint 5.4)
assertion_results.evidence: Reuse analysis states: 'No new utility functions or shared modules need to be created. The implementation is entirely composed of existing building blocks.' Plan calls apply_filter directly.
